codeReview.summary: The revocation fix is small and targeted: `TokenBlacklistService.alignIssuedAtAfterRevocation(...)` bumps only newly issued login/refresh timestamps whose millisecond bucket would otherwise collide with a stored revocation marker, and `AuthService` now consistently uses the aligned issue time for both access-token minting and refresh-token issuance. Existing fail-closed checks remain unchanged for older tokens because revocation still compares the original consumed refresh-token issue time on refresh and exact instants on bearer validation; the added unit/service coverage plus compose-backed probes demonstrate the intended boundary behavior—old sessions are rejected after logout while immediate fresh sessions stay usable, and masked foreign-tenant admin denials still preserve the existing `User not found` contract.

summary: PASS. Immediate post-revocation relogins are preserved without reopening stale-session access or cross-tenant incident-response regressions.
